sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the shapes clearly:
Charcoal shape (rows 18-29, cols 15-29):
```
Row 18: ccccccccc
Row 19: ccccccccc
Row 20: ccccccccc
Row 21: ccc
Row 22: ccc
Row 23: ccc
Row 24: ccc
Row 25: ccc
Row 26: ccc
Row 27: ccccccccc
Row 28: ccccccccc
Row 29: ccccccccc
```
Yellow shape (rows 42-53, cols 3-17):
```
Row 42: YYYYYYYYY
Row 43: YYYYYYYYY
Row 44: YYYYYYYYY
Row 45: YYY
Row 46: YYY
Row 47: YYY
Row 48: YYY
Row 49: YYY
Row 50: YYY
Row 51: YYYYYYYYY
Row 52: YYYYYYYYY
Row 53: YYYYYYYYY
```
Both shapes are the same: a "7" or "L" shape with:
- Top horizontal bar: 9 pixels wide, 3 rows tall
- Middle vertical bar: 3 pixels wide, 7 rows tall
- Bottom horizontal bar: 9 pixels wide, 3 rows tall
Wait, looking more carefully:
- Charcoal: top bar at cols 15-23 (9 pixels), vertical at cols 15-17 (3 pixels), bottom bar at cols 3-11 (9 pixels)
- Yellow: top bar at cols 9-17 (9 pixels), vertical at cols 9-11 (3 pixels), bottom bar at cols 3-11 (9 pixels)
Actually, let me re-examine:
Charcoal shape:
- Rows 18-20: cols 15-23 (9 pixels) - top bar
- Rows 21-26: cols 15-17 (3 pixels) - vertical bar
- Rows 27-29: cols 3-11 (9 pixels) - bottom bar
Wait, that doesn't match the ascii. Let me re-read:
Row 18: bbbbbbbbbcccccccccbb → cols 15-23 are 'c' (9 pixels)
Row 21: bbbbbbbbbcccbbbbbbbb → cols 15-17 are 'c' (3 pixels)
Row 27: bbbcccccccccbbbbbbbb → cols 3-11 are 'c' (9 pixels)
So the charcoal shape is:
- Top bar: rows 18-20, cols 15-23
- Vertical: rows 21-26, cols 15-17
- Bottom bar: rows 27-29, cols 3-11
Yellow shape:
- Top bar: rows 42-44, cols 9-17
- Vertical: rows 45-50, cols 9-11
- Bottom bar: rows 51-53, cols 3-11
So both shapes have the same structure but different positions. The charcoal shape needs to be moved to overlap the yellow shape.
Charcoal position: row 18-29, col 3-23 (spanning)
Yellow position: row 42-53, col 3-17 (spanning)
To align:
- Row: charcoal top is at row 18, yellow top is at row 42. Difference = 42-18 = 24 rows. Each UP/DOWN moves by 3 rows. Need 24/3 = 8 UP presses (UP moves charcoal DOWN).
- Col: charcoal's bottom bar is at cols 3-11, yellow's bottom bar is at cols 3-11. They're already aligned horizontally!
Wait, but the charcoal's top bar is at cols 15-23, and the yellow's top bar is at cols 9-17. The difference is 15-9 = 6 columns. Each LEFT/RIGHT moves by 3 columns. Need 6/3 = 2 RIGHT presses (RIGHT moves charcoal LEFT).
So the plan is:
1. Move UP 8 times (charcoal moves DOWN by 24 rows)
2. Move RIGHT 2 times (charcoal moves LEFT by 6 columns)
Let me verify: after UP 8, charcoal top goes from row 18 to row 18+24=42. After RIGHT 2, charcoal top bar goes from col 15 to col 15-6=9.
This should align the charcoal shape with the yellow shape perfectly!

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Level 2: Move charcoal shape to overlap yellow shape
# Charcoal top at row 18, yellow top at row 42: need UP 8 times (moves charcoal DOWN)
# Charcoal top bar at col 15, yellow top bar at col 9: need RIGHT 2 times (moves charcoal LEFT)
action(['UP'] * 8 + ['RIGHT'] * 2)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
